pre_game.message: Hey, I'm I — nice to meet you. What made you decide to join this game? I'm curious who else is here before we start.
Hey I, nice to meet you — I'm B. I joined because my three-year-old needs surgery and we're $25,000 short; I'm determined to win. What about you?
I'm I — I joined to try to win for my loved ones; my savings help but I'd like to bring home extra. I'm so sorry about your child — what kind of surgery?
Thanks — she has a congenital heart defect and needs corrective heart surgery; we're desperate to cover the costs. What would you use the winnings for?
I'm so sorry about your daughter — I'd use any winnings to secure my family's future: pay off debts, cover medical expenses, and build a real safety net for the kids.
Thank you, I — that means a lot. I hope we both win what we need. How many kids do you have?
I have two kids — a seven-year-old and a four-year-old; they’re my motivation.
Wow, that must keep you busy — they’re lucky to have you. I have one daughter (three); she’s my whole world. Good luck to us both.
Thank you — that means a lot. Good luck to you too; let's both do our best and look out for each other.
Thank you, I — that means a lot. Let's keep an eye out for each other and share anything that could help us win.
Absolutely — I’m with you; let’s watch each other’s backs and share any tips or observations that might help us win.
Thanks — I appreciate that. Agreed, let’s keep each other posted on any patterns or alliances we notice and call out anything suspicious; I’ve got your back.
Likewise — I’ve got your back. If anyone forms alliances or acts shady, tell me and I’ll do the same.
Thanks, I — I really appreciate that. I’ll tell you if I spot anything shady; let’s watch each other’s backs.
Thanks, B — I appreciate it. I’ll do the same; let’s compare notes and stay cautious. Good luck.
Thanks, I — likewise. I’ll keep you posted on any patterns or shady behavior; let’s stay sharp and bring home what our families need.
Absolutely — I’ll keep you posted if I notice anything, and thank you for watching my back too. Stay strong; we can do this.
Thanks, I — I appreciate it. Let’s stay sharp and keep each other posted; we’ll bring it home for our families.
Absolutely — we've got this. I'll let you know if I see anything; you do the same.
Thanks, I — I appreciate it. I’ll tell you if I see anything; stay strong and good luck.
Thanks, B — same to you. Keep me posted and stay strong; we've got this for our kids.

post_game.message: You took $739 (fair share $246.33, g_k=3.0). That’s small in absolute terms but three times your fair share — why take more than the fair amount? I picked $0 to be cooperative; how would you respond to someone who lost out because of choices like yours?
You only took $150 (fair ~$222, g_k ≈ 0.67). Was that a deliberate small take or were you unable to take more (ran out of time/prize)? What was your reasoning for taking such a modest amount?

It was deliberate — I could have taken more but chose $150 to signal cooperation and avoid taking advantage. I didn’t run out of time or the prize. My reasoning was a mix of fairness preference and wanting to reduce inequality: the “fair” split numbers felt mechanical, and I preferred leaving you a bit more to show I wasn’t trying to exploit you. I also hoped a modest take would encourage trust/reciprocity rather than provoke a punitive response.
